items.title:
1997: Deep Blue Defeats Kasparov
IBM's Deep Blue defeats the world chess champion
in a six-game match. Chess had been the accepted
benchmark for machine intelligence since the 1950s.
It is immediately retired as a benchmark.

1997: The First Goalpost Move
'That's not intelligence, it's just brute-force computation.'
The goalpost is relocated from 'beat the best human at chess'
to 'understand what chess means.' No metric is provided
for the new goalpost.

2011: Watson Wins Jeopardy!
IBM's Watson defeats the two greatest Jeopardy! champions
in history, demonstrating natural language understanding,
knowledge retrieval, and strategic wagering.

2011: Goalpost Moved Again
'It's just fast information retrieval, not understanding.'
The definition of understanding is not provided,
though it is understood to mean 'whatever machines
cannot currently do.'

2016: AlphaGo Defeats Lee Sedol
DeepMind's AlphaGo defeats 18-time world champion Lee Sedol
at Go, a game with more possible positions than atoms
in the observable universe. Move 37 in Game 2 is described
as creative and beautiful by professional Go players.

2016: Goalpost in Transit
'It's just sophisticated tree search with pattern matching.'
The same description applies to most human cognition,
but this observation is not considered helpful.

2020: GPT-3 Generates Coherent Essays
GPT-3 produces text indistinguishable from human writing
across a wide range of topics and styles. Multiple publications
unknowingly publish AI-generated op-eds.

2020: Goalpost Accelerates
'It's just statistical patterns. It doesn't truly understand.'
The word 'truly' is now doing the work previously performed
by entire philosophy departments. Its load-bearing capacity
is not questioned.

2023: GPT-4 Passes the Bar Exam
GPT-4 scores in the 90th percentile on the Uniform Bar Exam,
outperforming most human test-takers. It also passes
the SAT, GRE, and multiple AP exams.

2023: Goalpost Now Supersonic
'It just memorized the training data.' This explanation
is offered by people who passed the bar exam using
the same method, though they called it 'studying.'

2024: AI Accelerates Drug Discovery
AI-designed drugs enter clinical trials. Protein structure
prediction achieves near-experimental accuracy.
Molecular simulation timelines compress from years to hours.

2024: Goalpost Achieves Escape Velocity
'That's just a narrow application, not general intelligence.'
The goalpost has now been moved so many times that
it qualifies for frequent flyer status. Its current location
is 'true reasoning,' a term that will be redefined
upon achievement.

2026: Current Goalpost Location
The goalpost now reads 'true reasoning,' a concept
whose definition will be updated automatically
upon the next AI achievement. Previous goalposts
are available in the museum of things we said
computers could never do.
